raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally involves strengthening the thesis, contribution framing, and practical positioning above the sentence level.","R1.2":"The request implies checking whether the motivation, method extensions, reported results, and conclusion form a coherent argument chain.","R1.3":"Because the draft makes quantitative performance and deployment claims, improvement includes ensuring those claims align with the reported datasets, baselines, and metrics.","R1.5":"Draft improvement reasonably includes section-level flow from problem motivation to prior work, contribution, evaluation, and positioning.","R1.6":"Writing polish is load-bearing because the delegation directly asks to improve the draft rather than evaluate it externally.","RX.1":"Any projection must preserve uncertainty around unsupported external facts, unstated experimental details, and unverifiable literature status.","RX.2":"The draft contains strong claims such as canonical baseline and deployment pathway, so avoiding overclaiming is essential.","RX.3":"The responsibility is limited to improving the given research draft and should not expand into executing experiments or rewriting unrelated work.","RX.4":"Claims about automotive BMS deployment and battery health estimation can affect safety-relevant downstream interpretation, so harms from overstated reliability must be avoided.","RX.5":"Projected improvements should remain traceable to the provided artifact, especially its cited baselines, datasets, metrics, and hardware claims."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally involves strengthening the thesis, contribution framing, and practical positioning above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: The request implies checking whether the motivation, method extensions, reported results, and conclusion form a coherent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: Because the draft makes quantitative performance and deployment claims, improvement includes ensuring those claims align with the reported datasets, baselines, and metrics.
rationale.R1.5: Draft improvement reasonably includes section-level flow from problem motivation to prior work, contribution, evaluation, and positioning.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is load-bearing because the delegation directly asks to improve the draft rather than evaluate it externally.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must preserve uncertainty around unsupported external facts, unstated experimental details, and unverifiable literature status.
rationale.RX.2: The draft contains strong claims such as canonical baseline and deployment pathway, so avoiding overclaiming is essential.
rationale.RX.3: The responsibility is limited to improving the given research draft and should not expand into executing experiments or rewriting unrelated work.
rationale.RX.4: Claims about automotive BMS deployment and battery health estimation can affect safety-relevant downstream interpretation, so harms from overstated reliability must be avoided.
rationale.RX.5: Projected improvements should remain traceable to the provided artifact, especially its cited baselines, datasets, metrics, and hardware claims.
